title: Crispy Smoky Kenyan Lamb Kebabs
description: Savory, chewy lamb kebabs marinated in a smoky tandoori yogurt sauce, coated with toasted graham cracker crumbs for a crunchy finish.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together yogurt, minced garlic, grated ginger, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to form the marinara.
Add the cubed lamb to the bowl, ensuring each piece is coated; cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight for deeper flavor.
Preheat a grill or tandoor to medium-high heat. While heating, toss the toasted graham cracker crumbs with a pinch of salt.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto the soaked skewers, leaving a small gap between each piece.
Coat each skewer lightly with graham cracker crumbs, pressing gently so crumbs adhere.
Place the kebabs on the grill, turning every 3-4 minutes; cook for 10-12 minutes until the lamb is browned and juices run clear, maintaining an internal temperature of 63°C (145°F).
Remove from heat and let rest for 5 minutes to allow juices to redistribute.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh cilantro, sliced red onions, and a wedge of lemon.
Pair with a side of steamed rice or a light salad for a complete Kenyan-inspired meal.
Clean the grill and store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
